Welcome to NorthPark

Who We Are &
What We Believe

Our heart for Port Colborne

A warm community
in Port Colborne

We value family, community and discipleship. We are a loving community being transformed by God, serving Port Colborne, the Niagara region and the world.

Our Mission: Building passionate followers of Jesus Christ to go and make disciples.

Our Mission & Vision →About PAOC →MCS College Hub →

How We Live It Out

Our Values

These values shape everything we do, from Sunday mornings to midweek ministries to how we serve our neighbours.

The Bible

We value the Bible, believing it to be God's message to the world.

Community & Family

We value community and family, as the church operates like a family caring for the physical, spiritual, social, and psychological needs of one another.

Caring for Our Community

We value caring for the community by reaching out with love to the lost and those in need.

Generosity

We value generosity through sacrificial giving.

Every Generation & Nation

We value all generations and nationalities of people.

Growth

We value growth, believing that God's desire for the church and His people is to multiply.

What we believe

Our Faith & Values

  • We believe in the Holy Trinity, Father, Son, and Holy Spirit.
  • We believe the Bible is the inspired and authoritative Word of God.
  • We believe in salvation through faith in Jesus Christ alone.
  • We believe in water baptism as a public declaration of faith.
  • We believe in the baptism of the Holy Spirit with the evidence of speaking in tongues.
  • We believe in divine healing through the power of Jesus Christ.
  • We believe in the imminent, physical return of Jesus Christ.
  • We are affiliated with the Pentecostal Assemblies of Canada (PAOC).
  • We partner with MCS Bible College as a local hub for theological training.

The people who lead us

Meet Our Leaders

Youth Ministry

Pathfinders
Youth Group

← Back to All Ministries

Every Wednesday Evening

Built for Teens

Pathfinders is our high-energy, Spirit-filled youth group for teens. Every Wednesday evening from 6:30 to 8:00 PM we gather for worship, real talk, games, and genuine community.

Keep an eye out for our special Pathfinders events held on select Fridays throughout the year!

We believe in young people, in their faith, their gifts, and their ability to make a difference right now. Pathfinders is a place where teens can ask hard questions, build lasting friendships, and grow in their walk with Jesus.

Get Involved

When

Every Wednesday Evening
6:30 PM – 8:30 PM

Who

Students in Grades 7–12
All are welcome

Where

484 Barrick Rd
Port Colborne, ON

Children's Ministry

Kids Pac
Program

← Back to All Ministries

Every Wednesday Evening

Fun for Kids

Kids Pac is our Wednesday evening program for children from JK to Grade 7, packed with Bible stories, crafts, games, and the kind of friendship that lasts a lifetime. We meet every Wednesday from 6:30 to 8:00 PM.

We love kids and want every child to know they are seen, valued, and loved by God. Kids Pac runs alongside adult Bible Study so the whole family can come together on Wednesday nights.

Get Involved

When

Every Wednesday Evening
6:30 PM – 8:00 PM

Who

Children in Grades K–6
All are welcome

Also on Sunday

Super Kids Church
During the 10:30 AM service

Men's Ministry

Men's
Ministry

← Back to All Ministries

Regular Gatherings

Brotherhood in Christ

Men's Ministry is a place for men of all ages to build authentic friendships, hold each other accountable, and grow together in faith and purpose.

We gather regularly for fellowship, prayer, and study, covering topics relevant to men's lives, families, and faith. Whether you've been a Christian for decades or are just starting to explore, you're welcome here.

Connect With Us

You might also be interested in

Women's Ministry →Bible Study →Sunday Services →All Ministries →Contact Us →

Women's Ministry

Women's
Ministry

← Back to All Ministries

Regular Gatherings

Sisterhood & Community

Women's Ministry is a welcoming space for women at every stage of life, to be known, encouraged, and to grow together in faith.

We meet for Bible study, prayer, and community events throughout the year. Our heart is for every woman to feel at home, to discover her gifts, and to walk confidently in God's purpose for her life.

Secret Sister: a year-long encouragement program pairing women together to bless one another through small gifts, notes, and prayer.

Connect With Us

Adult Ministry

Bible Study

← Back to All Ministries

Every Wednesday Evening

Going Deeper

Midweek Bible Study is a warm, discussion-based gathering for adults who want to go deeper into God's Word together.

We work through books of the Bible, ask honest questions, and apply Scripture to everyday life. No prior knowledge required, just a desire to learn and grow. Kids Pac and Pathfinders run at the same time, so bring the whole family.

Join Us Wednesday

When

Every Wednesday Evening
6:30 PM – 8:00 PM

Who

Adults of all ages
All are welcome

Also

Wednesday Morning Prayer
11:00 AM

High School Ministry

Rooted
Youth Group

← Back to All Ministries

Port Colborne High School

Put Down Roots

Rooted is our youth group specifically connecting with Port Colborne high school students, helping young people find their identity, community, and purpose in Christ.

High school is full of big questions. Rooted is a safe place to ask them. We meet, talk honestly, worship together, and build the kind of friendships that help teens stand firm in their faith.

Get Connected

You might also be interested in

Pathfinders Youth →Wednesday Night →Sunday Services →All Ministries →Contact Us →

Discipleship

First Things
First

← Back to All Ministries

New & Growing Believers

Beginning Your Journey

First Things First is a foundational discipleship program designed for those who are new to faith, or who want to revisit the essentials of following Jesus.

We cover the basics: who God is, what the Bible says, how to pray, and what it means to live as a follower of Jesus. It's practical, welcoming, and a great next step for anyone exploring Christian faith.

Find Out More

Summer Program

Weeklong
Day Camp

← Back to All Ministries

Summer 2026

The Best Week of Summer

Our weeklong Day Camp is a highlight of the summer for kids in JK through Grade 8. Games, sports, dramas, crafts, snacks, a trip to Safari Niagara, and tons of fun, all in a safe and caring environment built on faith and friendship.

Choose either Week 1 (July 6 to 10) or Week 2 (July 13 to 17). Spots are limited to 50 kids per week, so early registration is encouraged.

Register Online Printable Form

When & Who

Dates & Schedule

Week 1

July 6 to 10
9:00 AM to 4:00 PM daily

Week 2

July 13 to 17
9:00 AM to 4:00 PM daily

Who

Children JK to Grade 8
Limit of 50 kids per week

Safari Niagara Trip

July 9 or 16
$25 per accompanying adult

Registration Pricing

Register Early & Save

Cash only, please. Registration takes place Wednesdays from 6:30 to 8:00 PM throughout April, or call to arrange a time.

Early Bird

March 1 to May 30

$100 / child

Regular

June 1 to June 30

$110 / child

Late

July 1 to July 4

$125 / child

Get Ready for Summer

How to Register

1. Fill Out the Form

Complete the registration form online below, or print and fill out the PDF version.

2. We Confirm Your Spot

Our team will be in touch. If you'd rather drop off a paper form, come by Wednesday evenings (6:30 to 8:00 PM) in April.

3. Pay (Cash Only)

Bring exact cash for your registration tier at drop-off or camp check-in.

Want to Volunteer?

Volunteer opportunities are available, including community hours for high school students. Contact us to join the team.

Get in Touch

Register Online

Complete Your Registration

Fill out the form below and submit it directly to our office. We'll be in touch to confirm your spot. Payment (cash only) is collected when you drop off or at camp check-in.

Prefer paper? Download the printable PDF form instead.

Choose Your Week

Limit of 50 kids per week. Please select one.

Child Information

Parent / Guardian Information

Emergency Contact

This should be someone other than the parent/guardian listed above, in case they cannot be reached.

Health & Safety

If your child has limitations requiring one-on-one care, please send a care person with your child, as we do not have volunteers to accommodate this.

If yes, please request a medication form. Please understand that NorthPark Worship Centre does not dispense any medication to children.

Safari Niagara Trip

Children 7 and under must be accompanied by a parent/guardian. Please include $25.00 for yourself and any other family member attending.

Consent & Acknowledgement

Information received is confidential and is gathered for the purpose of serving your child while in the care of NorthPark Worship Centre (formerly Christian Life Assembly). Any medical information collected here authorizes NorthPark, its staff, and volunteers to obtain medical assistance in emergencies. The safety of your child is our primary concern, and precautions will be taken for their wellbeing and protection.

By submitting this form, I/we, the parent(s) or guardian(s) of the child named above, authorize the Senior Pastor, Assistant Pastor, or a member of NorthPark ministry staff to consent to medical treatment and to authorize any physician or hospital to provide medical assessment, treatment, or procedures for the participant named on this form. I/we agree to indemnify and hold blameless the Senior Pastor, Assistant Pastor, ministry staff, Board, and volunteers of NorthPark Worship Centre from and against any loss, damage, or injury suffered by the participant as a result of participating in NorthPark activities.

Photos: We will be taking photographs during the week for a slideshow presented on a Sunday service, and may post pictures on our church website and social media.

This form will be sent securely to the NorthPark office. Cash payment is collected separately, this form does not process payment.

Registration Received!

Thank you for registering for Day Camp 2026. Our team will be in touch to confirm your spot. Please remember that payment is cash only and is collected at drop-off or check-in.

Our Heart

Mission
& Vision

← Back to About Us

Why We Exist

Called to Love.
Sent to Serve.

Who we are: We value family, community and discipleship. We are a loving community being transformed by God, serving Port Colborne, the Niagara region and the world.

Our Mission: Building passionate followers of Jesus Christ to go and make disciples.

We pursue this by gathering for worship every Sunday, equipping believers through midweek ministries, and reaching our neighbours through compassionate outreach and global missions.

How We Live Out Our Mission

Reaching. Teaching. Sending.

Reaching in Love

We reach out to our community and world with the love of Christ.

Teaching the Truth

We teach the truth of God's Word, faithfully and clearly.

Sending in Power

We send believers out in the power of the Holy Spirit to make disciples.

Our Values

What We Stand For

  • We value the Bible, believing it to be God's message to the world.
  • We value community and family, as the church operates like a family caring for the physical, spiritual, social and psychological needs of one another.
  • We value caring for the community by reaching out with love to the lost and those who are in need.
  • We value generosity through sacrificial giving.
  • We value all generations and nationalities of people.
  • We value growth, believing that God's desire for the church and His people is to multiply.

Leadership Team →What We Believe →PAOC →MCS College →About Us →Contact Us →

Our Team

Leadership
Team

The people who lead us

Faithful Servants of Christ

Our leadership team is committed to God, to one another, and to the people of Port Colborne. Guided by Scripture and empowered by the Holy Spirit, they serve with humility and joy.

We have an elected board who work alongside the Lead Pastor and staff to accomplish God's calling for our local church.

Doctrine & Theology

What We
Believe

← Back to About Us

Our Statement of Faith

Our Core Beliefs

As a Pentecostal church affiliated with the PAOC, our faith is rooted in Scripture and expressed in the following core beliefs.

  • We believe in one God, eternally existing in three persons: Father, Son, and Holy Spirit.
  • We believe the Bible is the inspired, infallible, and authoritative Word of God.
  • We believe in the deity of Jesus Christ, His virgin birth, sinless life, atoning death, bodily resurrection, and ascension.
  • We believe salvation comes through repentance and faith in Jesus Christ alone, not by works.
  • We believe in water baptism by immersion as a public declaration of faith.
  • We believe in the baptism of the Holy Spirit with the initial evidence of speaking in other tongues.
  • We believe in the ongoing gifts of the Holy Spirit for the Church today.
  • We believe in divine healing through the redemptive work of Christ.
  • We believe in the imminent, physical, and personal return of Jesus Christ.
  • We believe in the resurrection of the dead, eternal life for the saved, eternal separation for the lost.

Read the official PAOC statement of faith at paoc.org/family/what-we-believe

You might also be interested in

Mission & Vision →Leadership Team →PAOC →Sunday Services →About Us →

Our Affiliation

Pentecostal Assemblies
of Canada

← Back to About Us

PAOC

Part of Something Larger

NorthPark Worship Centre is proudly affiliated with the Pentecostal Assemblies of Canada, one of the country's largest Evangelical denominations.

The PAOC is a fellowship of over 1,100 churches and 250,000 adherents across Canada, united by a common faith, a commitment to the authority of Scripture, and the empowering work of the Holy Spirit.

Our affiliation connects us to a national network of churches, mission workers, and ministry resources, so that the work we do locally is part of a much larger story of God's kingdom advancing across Canada and around the world.

Visit PAOC.org Western Ontario District

You might also be interested in

Mission & Vision →MCS College →What We Believe →About Us →

Theological Education

MCS Bible
College Hub

← Back to About Us

Local Training, National College

Grow Deeper
in Your Faith

NorthPark serves as a local hub for Master's College and Seminary, bringing accredited theological education right here to Port Colborne.

MCS offers degree and certificate programs in ministry, theology, and leadership, designed for those who want to deepen their knowledge of Scripture and grow in their calling, whether for vocational ministry or personal growth.

As a local hub, NorthPark makes it possible to access quality theological education without leaving your community. Contact us to find out what's currently available.

Visit MCS.edu Ask Us About It

Missions

Our Global Partners

Reaching the
Nations

NorthPark partners with and financially supports mission workers around the world, helping advance the gospel beyond our borders.

Ed Dickson, Loads of Love

Ukraine

Humanitarian aid and gospel ministry in Ukraine. Ed Dickson brings practical relief and the hope of Christ to families affected by conflict.

Jonathan & Sarah Lublinkov

Local Outreach

Home Missions
& Outreach

← Back to All Missions

Serving Port Colborne

Loving Our
Neighbours

We believe the church exists for its community. Here are some of the ways NorthPark serves the people of Port Colborne and the surrounding area.

Port Cares Food Bank

Supporting local families with food security. We regularly collect and donate to the Port Cares Food Bank.

Lions Food Drive

Community food collection in partnership with the local Lions Club, keeping pantries stocked for those in need.

Canadian Blood Services

Our congregation supports life-saving blood donation drives, one of the most direct ways to help save lives.

blood.ca →

South Niagara Life Center

A regional outreach partner serving vulnerable community members across South Niagara.

Clothes for Missions

Collecting and distributing clothing to those in need, locally in Port Colborne and globally through our mission partners.

Joining Us Online
